intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A warm, supportive tone with gentle nudges will feel more motivating for long-term health tracking than a neutral clinical tone
facade.axisTargeted: VOICE AND PERSONALITY — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Encouraging coach voice
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers personal, supportive communication that celebrates progress and offers positive reinforcement over data
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers neutral, objective tone that stays out of the way and lets the data speak for itself

evidence.hypothesis: A warm, supportive tone with gentle nudges will feel more motivating for long-term health tracking than a neutral clinical tone
evidence.implication: User prefers personal, supportive communication that celebrates progress and offers positive reinforcement over data

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments respond better to warm, soft lighting and organic color palettes that feel nurturing rather than clinical
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use warm accent colors (amber, soft orange), rounded corners, soft shadows, and a sense of comfort and care in visual hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler, crisper aesthetics—move toward clean whites, cool grays, sharp typography, and a more clinical or technical visual language

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard with gentle, curved UI elements and subtle shadows will feel more approachable and less clinical than sharp-edged alternatives
facade.axisTargeted: Roundness vs. sharpness in component geometry
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: User may prefer organic, friendly aesthetics over minimalist or brutalist designs; curved forms signal calm and safety in health contexts
facade.rejectImplies: User may gravitate toward crisp, modern, geometric designs; prefers clarity and precision in data presentation over softer visual language

facade.hypothesis: Users managing multiple health experiments need persistent navigation that doesn't compete with content—a fixed sidebar paired with scrollable card layouts keeps experiments scannable without overwhelming the main view
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User wants persistent, organized navigation + prefers cards (grouped info chunks) over lists; likely values quick switching between experiments and visual hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: User finds sidebars cramping or cards redundant; may prefer tabs, full-width dense tables, or a different navigation paradigm entirely for experiments dashboard

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer playful, elastic transitions when dashboard views load, over instant reveals
facade.label: Bounce on open
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use spring-based animations for metric cards, charts, and section reveals. Feels forgiving and alive.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer snappy, direct interactions. Dashboard should reveal content instantly or with linear timing. Feels efficient and controlled.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users track long-term health experiments better with metric-focused displays than narrative summaries
facade.label: Numbers over paragraphs
facade.acceptImplies: Show quantified progress (weight, sleep hours, steps) as the primary view; reserve explanatory text for drill-down only
facade.rejectImplies: Lead with narrative context and interpretation; numbers are supporting detail, not the hero

facade.hypothesis: A clean, numbers-focused interface with minimal encouragement language will appeal to users who prefer objectivity and self-direction over motivational framing
facade.axisTargeted: tone: motivational support vs data-centric objectivity
facade.label: Data-first, minimal
facade.acceptImplies: User values quantified progress and autonomy; prefers interfaces that present facts without emotional scaffolding
facade.rejectImplies: User finds pure data presentation cold or unmotivating; needs narrative and encouragement woven into tracking experience

draft.title: HealthLab Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments with experiment cards, metrics overview, and quick-log interface.
